get.description: Returns the login methods of the ACTIVE admin-auth strategies (env ADMIN_AUTH, ordered). Deliberately unauthenticated: the login page needs it before any credential exists.
get.summary: Login-method discovery for the admin UI (unauthenticated)

post.summary: Create a document (one per type × audience)

get.summary: List documents (flat) including the current PUBLISHED version DTO (or null)
Version history of a document — every entry carries a presigned pdfUrl

post.summary: Create a DRAFT version (multipart/form-data primary; base64 JSON fallback)

patch.summary: Patch a DRAFT version (metadata and/or replacement PDF)

get.description: Number of customers the publish rollout would target (customers whose roles include the document audience). Lets the admin UI show the impact next to the publish button before a DRAFT is published (issue #27).
get.summary: How many customers publishing this version would affect

get.description: Every row carries a compliance indicator (compliant + complianceStatus) computed over the customer's states and the current published versions. `audience`/`documentType` NARROW the list to the customers who actually have a matching role/document assigned (and also scope the per-row indicator); an unknown key matches nothing → empty list. `compliance` additionally filters the rows. search + narrowing + compliance filter run before pagination, so `total` is the filtered count.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on name, externalRef and contactEmails. Applied before pagination; `total` reflects the filtered count.

parameters.name: audience
parameters.description: Audience key — NARROWS the list to customers whose roles include this audience (and scopes the per-row compliance indicator to that audience). Unknown key → empty list.

parameters.name: documentType
parameters.description: Document type key — NARROWS the list to customers who have a document of this type assigned (a document whose audience matches one of the customer's roles) and scopes the per-row compliance indicator to that type. Unknown key → empty list.

parameters.name: compliance
parameters.description: Keep only customers whose (scoped) compliance matches this status.

post.description: Roles are validated against the audiences; a role change takes effect on the next publish/rollout only (no auto-rollout here). Optional `acceptedVersions` records already signed versions as IMPORT acceptances. externalRef is unique only among customers with OVERLAPPING roles — the partner and customer external ID spaces are separate, so the same externalRef may coexist on records with disjoint roles; a duplicate that shares at least one role → 422 INVALID_STATE.

422.description: INVALID_STATE (externalRef duplicate sharing a role, invalid e-mail) · UNKNOWN_AUDIENCE · ROLE_MISMATCH

get.description: The full customer record (id, externalRef, firstName, lastName, companyName, roles, contactEmails) — e.g. for the detail-page header.
get.summary: Get a single customer by id

patch.description: A `roles` change takes effect on the next publish/rollout only (no auto-rollout on role add). Adding a role is rejected with 422 INVALID_STATE if it would overlap another customer that already shares this externalRef.

422.description: INVALID_STATE (invalid e-mail, externalRef overlap on role add) · UNKNOWN_AUDIENCE

post.description: Returns `${PUBLIC_BASE_URL}/accept/<token>` — a capability URL the admin sends directly to the person who has to accept (no portal integration needed). The raw token appears only in this response; the server persists just its SHA-256. Optional `audienceKey` scopes the page to one audience; `expiresInDays` defaults to 30 (max 365). Writes an ACCEPTANCE_LINK_CREATE audit entry.

422.description: INVALID_STATE (PUBLIC_BASE_URL unset, expiresInDays out of range) · UNKNOWN_AUDIENCE

post.summary: Mint a hosted acceptance link for a customer

post.description: Retires the previous version, supersedes its open customer states, creates PENDING_NOTIFICATION states for all customers with a matching role and triggers e-mails. Scheduled effectiveness: with a FUTURE validFrom the rollout still happens immediately (advance acceptance), but the previous version stays PUBLISHED — it remains the compliance baseline until the flip at validFrom, when the activation sweeper retires it and supersedes its open states.
post.summary: Publish a DRAFT version (immutable afterwards) + rollout

get.description: One entry per RELEVANT version (the current published version and, if any, the upcoming scheduled published version of every document) with acceptance counters: totalCustomers (relevant/non-SUPERSEDED states), accepted, acceptedByChannel/Method, pending, blocked, objected and acceptanceRate.
get.summary: Per-version acceptance dashboard — current + upcoming published versions
Acceptance counters for a single version

get.description: Every row reports the customer state and acceptance FOR THIS version (not the currently effective one) — so drilling into an upcoming version shows who has (not) accepted THAT version. SUPERSEDED states are excluded; `acceptance` is the effective acceptance of this version only. `stats` reuses the dashboard per-version counters so the header matches the card.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring on the customer name / externalRef / contactEmails.

get.summary: Full customer history incl. evidence and rollout states

post.summary: Manual (back-dated) acceptance recording — reason + evidence required

patch.summary: Extend a deadline / suspend a block (reason required)

get.description: Read-only — audiences are managed via the legal-entities configuration file.
Read-only — document types are managed via the legal-entities configuration file.
get.summary: List document types (sorted by key) incl. e-mail template assignments
List e-mail templates (sorted by name; default rows flagged)

patch.summary: Update an e-mail template (default rows are editable)

422.description: INVALID_STATE (default template, or still assigned to a document type)

delete.summary: Delete an e-mail template (default rows and assigned ones are refused)

post.summary: Render a template with realistic sample values (subject/html/text)
Upload an externally-signed document for a customer (multipart; base64 JSON fallback)

get.summary: List a customer’s signed documents (newest first)

302.description: Redirect to a fresh presigned PDF URL (time-limited).

get.summary: Download a signed document PDF (302 → presigned URL)

get.description: A single newest-first event list for legal tracing — for the whole system or one customer — read from the append-only Event table the core writes on each successful action (dual-write alongside the unchanged evidence stores). Categories: COMMUNICATION (e-mail sent/delivered), ACCESS (hosted acceptance page opened), CONSENT (acceptances + objections), ADMINISTRATION (all admin/system actions). All filters run BEFORE pagination, so `total` is the filtered count. A date-only `to` is treated as end-of-day.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Inclusive lower bound on occurredAt (ISO date-time; a date-only value = start of that day).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Inclusive upper bound on occurredAt (ISO date-time; a date-only value = END of that day).

get.summary: Chronological legal event / audit log (append-only Event table, filterable, 50/page)

info.title: clickwrap-server — admin API
info.description: Administration surface (`/admin/**`): documents & versions, publish/rollout, customers, operations (per-version dashboard, history, manual acceptance, deadlines/reminders) and the dynamic entities (audiences, document types). Errors are always `{ code, message }` — the codes are documented in docs/API.md §7.

google-sso.description: Google ID token (Google SSO; domain/allowlist checked server-side).

admin-token.name: x-admin-token
admin-token.description: Static admin token fallback (dev/CI; ADMIN_API_TOKEN). Optional: x-admin-user names the actor.

key.description: Plugin key of the admin-auth strategy.

flow.description: How the login page obtains a credential: 'google' = Google Identity Services with params.clientId (send the ID token as Authorization: Bearer); 'token' = static token prompt (send as x-admin-token); 'oidc-redirect' = redirect to params.authorizeUrl, the returned access token is sent as Authorization: Bearer.

params.description: Flow parameters — google: { clientId }, token: {}, oidc-redirect: { authorizeUrl, clientId? }. The Google clientId comes from the BACKEND env (GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID); a frontend-side VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID is obsolete.

message.example: A customer with externalRef "ext-1" and an overlapping role already exists

objectionPeriodDays.description: PASSIVE only: objection period in days.

gracePeriodDays.description: Deprecated: no longer drives ACTIVE blocking (legacy rows only).

hardDeadlineAt.description: ACTIVE only: absolute acceptance deadline. Every customer must accept by then or is blocked, independent of access.

pdfUrl.description: Presigned, time-limited PDF download/preview URL (15-minute TTL).

upcomingVersions.description: ALL UPCOMING published versions (validFrom in the future, scheduled publish), ordered by validFrom ascending (the nearest flip first). Empty when none are scheduled. Several future versions may be scheduled simultaneously — every one is listed, not just the next. The current version stays the compliance baseline until the flip at the nearest one's validFrom.

latestPdfUrl.description: Stable public URL (`${PUBLIC_BASE_URL}/documents/<type>/<audience>/latest.pdf`) that always 302-redirects to the currently effective published PDF — deterministic from the document keys, valid across future publishes (for rendering into offers). Null when no published version is in effect or PUBLIC_BASE_URL is unset.

file.description: PDF (multipart field) or base64 string (JSON fallback).

fileName.description: Required with the base64 JSON fallback.

objectionPeriodDays.description: PASSIVE only: objection period in days.

gracePeriodDays.description: Deprecated: no longer drives ACTIVE blocking (legacy rows only).

hardDeadlineAt.description: ACTIVE only: absolute acceptance deadline as a full ISO date-time. Required to publish an ACTIVE version and must be >= validFrom; every customer must accept by then or is blocked.

validFrom.description: ISO date from which the revision applies. May lie in the FUTURE (scheduled effectiveness): publishing rolls out immediately for advance acceptance, while the previous version stays the compliance baseline until this date.

audience.description: Document audience whose members would be rolled out to.

count.description: Number of customers that publishing this version would roll out to — customers whose roles include the document audience (the same set the publish rollout targets). Lets an admin see the impact before publishing a draft.

hardDeadlineAt.description: ACTIVE only: absolute acceptance deadline (ISO date-time, >= validFrom).

firstName.description: Contact person's given name ('' when unknown).

lastName.description: Contact person's family name ('' when unknown).

companyName.description: Optional company/organisation name — preferred display label when set.

deletedAt.description: Set only on a customer that was soft-deleted by the customer sync (removed from the external source). The detail page badges it; such customers are excluded from the list and never blocking.

compliant.description: Compliance gate (domain semantics: false = blocked). Present on list rows only; scoped by the audience/documentType query params (which also narrow the list to assigned/role-matching customers).

complianceStatus.description: Compact per-row compliance status for the list chip. Present on list rows only.

acceptedAt.description: Signature date; backdating is allowed for IMPORT. Defaults to now.

effectiveDate.description: Contract signing date — the version of each listed type effective at this date is accepted.

documentTypes.description: Document type keys the contract covers.

acceptedVersions.description: Versions already accepted out-of-band (signed offer) — recorded as IMPORT acceptances.

signedDocuments.description: Accept documents by type at a signing date: the version of each listed document type that was effective at effectiveDate is recorded as an IMPORT acceptance (#29).

firstName.description: Contact person's given name ('' when unknown).

lastName.description: Contact person's family name ('' when unknown).

companyName.description: Optional company/organisation name — preferred display label when set.

deletedAt.description: Set only on a customer that was soft-deleted by the customer sync (removed from the external source). The detail page badges it; such customers are excluded from the list and never blocking.

compliant.description: Compliance gate (domain semantics: false = blocked). Present on list rows only; scoped by the audience/documentType query params (which also narrow the list to assigned/role-matching customers).

complianceStatus.description: Compact per-row compliance status for the list chip. Present on list rows only.

roles.description: Takes effect on the next publish/rollout.

audienceKey.description: Optional scope: restrict the hosted page to documents of this audience key.

url.description: Shareable URL (`${PUBLIC_BASE_URL}/accept/<token>`). The raw token appears ONLY here — the server persists just its SHA-256.

rolloutCustomers.description: Number of customers the rollout targeted.

items.description: One entry per current + upcoming published version of every document.

actorName.description: Display name of the accepting actor.

customerName.description: Derived display name: companyName if set, else the contact person's name ('' when unknown).

state.description: The CustomerVersionState value for THIS version.

acceptance.description: The effective acceptance OF THIS VERSION only.

stats.description: Reused per-version dashboard stats (header numbers match the card).

signedDocuments.description: Externally-signed documents (evidence archive) — never part of the compliance gate.

suspendBlock.description: true: EXPIRED_BLOCKING → NOTIFIED with a new deadlineAt (then required).

external.description: true = externally-signed document type (SignedDocument flow, no versions/publish/gate); false = clickwrap type. Set at creation only, immutable afterwards.

isDefault.description: Built-in default row (editable but not deletable).

documentTypeKey.description: Scope the sample documentType value to this key.

subject.example: clickwrap-server: new version of DPA — Customers

text.description: Plain-text part derived from the rendered HTML.

fileName.description: Fallback: PDF file name (only with the base64 `file` fallback).

documentTypeKey.description: DocumentTypeDef key — MUST be an external type.

audience.description: Audience key (validated to exist when given).

contentHash.description: SHA-256 over the PDF, computed host-side.

pdfUrl.description: Time-limited (presigned) download URL of the signed PDF.

channel.description: Delivery / acceptance channel of the source record.

total.description: Total number of events AFTER filtering (before pagination).
